blocks.text: Now let me run the DESeq2 analysis in R:

input.code:
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)

# Load counts and metadata
counts_file <- file.path(Sys.getenv('WORK_DIR'), 'counts_de.csv')
metadata_file <- file.path(Sys.getenv('WORK_DIR'), 'metadata_de.csv')

counts <- read.csv(counts_file, row.names=1, check.names=FALSE)
metadata <- read.csv(metadata_file, row.names=1)

cat("Counts matrix:", nrow(counts), "genes ×", ncol(counts), "cells\n")
cat("Metadata:\n")
print(table(metadata$cell_type))

# Ensure counts are integer matrix
counts <- as.matrix(counts)
mode(counts) <- "integer"

# Create DESeqDataSet
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts,
                               colData = metadata,
                               design = ~ cell_type)

cat("\nDESeqDataSet created\n")
cat("Design formula: ~ cell_type\n")
cat("Reference level (colData$cell_type):", levels(dds$cell_type)[1], "\n")

# Set reference level: Monocyte as reference
dds$cell_type <- relevel(dds$cell_type, ref = "Monocyte")
cat("Reference releveled to: Monocyte\n")
